_doc: Remote test nodes for CatchChallenger.
Loaded once by test/remote_build.py at module load — single source of truth for
testingremote.py, testingserver.py (Phase E), testingclient.py (remote-server),
and the parallel start_remote_builds() helper.
Per-node fields:
label             short id used in test names and resume keys
comment           free-text description (not parsed)
ssh.host/user/port
work_dir          temp path on the node where sources are rsynced and built;
removed after the test phase by cleanup_remote_node()
arch              i686 | x86_64 | mips2r2 | ...
os                debian-12 | debian-13 | gentoo | ...
real_hardware     true if not a VM/container
has_mold          true if the mold linker is installed (-fuse-ld=mold)
has_gui           true if Qt GUI modules are available (drives compile filter)
gpu_works         true if a working GPU is available on the node
client_run_mode   how to launch the client when target is this node:
'offscreen' = QT_QPA_PLATFORM=offscreen on local machine
'x11'       = SSH into the node, set DISPLAY, run client there
'none'      = do not run a client against this node
x11               only meaningful when client_run_mode='x11':
display          target X display (e.g. ':0')
session_user     unix user whose X session is being used
display_manager  sddm | trinity | gdm | lightdm | ...
cxx_version       array of C++ versions to build the node with; each value
is passed as -std=<value> on its own remote build pass.
compilers         which compilers are available on the node. Used to filter
diagnostic runs: --sanitize requires clang, --valgrind
requires gcc. Default when missing/empty: ["gcc"].
Eg: ["gcc"], ["gcc","clang"]
extra_defines     CatchChallenger DEFINES+= passed at qmake invocation
tests             which test phases this node opts into:
'compile'        compile-only tests (remote_build / testingremote)
'server-client'  remote server + client (Phase E in testingserver,
and the remote-server block in testingclient)
'solo'           build both clients (qtcpu800x600 + qtopengl) on
the node and run each with --autosolo to verify
the standalone savegame flow (testingclient)
lxc_rootfs        path to the LXC rootfs on the host (only for lxc nodes,
handy when fixing the container from outside)

nodes.label: pentium-m
nodes.comment: Real hardware with partial GPU support: only OpenGL ES 2.0 works (partial OpenGL). Only node where client tests can run on a real DISPLAY (no offscreen). The Trinity DE session is logged in as 'user' too — launch the client as a graphical process under DISPLAY :0.

nodes.label: atom-n455
nodes.comment: DISABLED — temporarily off (tests: []). Real hardware with working GPU: OpenGL 4.5 — 0ad and SuperTuxKart run fine, so 3D games appear to work. Compile + server-side tests work — launch the client as a graphical process under DISPLAY :0.

nodes.label: mips-lxc
nodes.comment: LXC container on the local host (replaces 2803:1920::3:440a). No Qt GUI, no mold, builds with -std=c++23, NOAUDIO + NOWEBSOCKET defines.

nodes.label: x86-lxc
nodes.comment: LXC container on the local host (replaces 982.vps.confiared.com). i686 with mold linker.
